suggestions.title: Fix sandcastle prompt args mismatch
suggestions.description: Your current `dan/sandcastle-integration` worktree adds `.sandcastle/main.mts`, but `review-prompt.md` expects `SOURCE_BRANCH` and `merge-prompt.md` expects `TASK_ID` even though the runner never passes them. That is a concrete local break that will stop the first full sandcastle loop.
suggestions.prompt: Fix the prompt argument mismatches in `.sandcastle` so `pnpm sandcastle` can complete a full plan, implement, review, and merge cycle. Update the runner and prompt templates consistently, then add a minimal smoke check or usage note if needed.

suggestions.title: Wire org-next auth redirect flow
suggestions.description: Recent commits added the auth app and the first org-next auth pages, but `apps/org-next/docs/README.md` still says the return/callback wiring is not implemented, and `src/routes/-auth.ts` currently sends users to the bare auth app URL instead of the documented `/authorize?...` contract. This looks like the main remaining user-facing hole in the new app.
suggestions.prompt: Implement the org-next auth redirect contract end to end: send users to the auth app with the right authorize params, handle the return or callback path in org-next, and update the org-next docs and tests to match.

suggestions.title: Cover campaign rename in Playwright
suggestions.description: Commit `7453f23` added the org-next campaign detail screen with inline rename, but `apps/org-next/tests/campaigns.spec.ts` still only covers listing campaigns and the create modal. Given how often your recent Codex threads were about Playwright regressions, this is a focused gap worth closing now.
suggestions.prompt: Add an org-next Playwright test for renaming a campaign from the campaign detail page, including the `updateCampaign` mock and assertions that the edited name persists after the router refresh.

suggestions.title: Build org-next PKCE callback path
suggestions.description: Today's thread says org-next is pivoting off the auth app and onto Cognito directly. In the repo, `apps/org-next/docs/README.md` still says the callback wiring is not implemented, and `src/routes/-auth.ts` still just redirects to `VITE_AUTH_APP_URL`, so the main missing artifact is the self-contained PKCE flow.
suggestions.prompt: Replace org-next's current auth-app redirect stub with a self-contained Cognito PKCE flow. Add the authorize redirect builder, callback route and state verifier, exchange the code at Cognito `/oauth2/token`, persist access and refresh tokens in the existing session cookies, and update the org-next docs and tests to match.

suggestions.title: Finish org-next logout flow
suggestions.description: Today's auth threads included logout in the target flow. In `apps/org-next`, `performLogout` still POSTs a bare `/logout`, but `apps/auth/docs/client-integration.md` requires `client_id` and `logout_uri`, and the authenticated UI does not expose a logout action yet.
suggestions.prompt: Implement the org-next logout flow end to end. Add a visible logout action in the authenticated UI, redirect through the auth app using the documented `/logout?client_id=...&logout_uri=...` contract, clear any org-next local session state that should be dropped, and cover the flow with tests plus doc updates in `apps/org-next/docs/README.md`.

suggestions.title: Make local auth integration runnable
suggestions.description: The latest org-next auth thread asked for exact local setup steps, and the repo still fights that: `apps/org-next/package.json` sets `VITE_API_HOSTNAME` instead of `API_HOSTNAME`, `.env.template` defaults to bypass auth, and the auth README allowlist example still points at `org.anedot.me` instead of a local org-next callback/logout URL.
suggestions.prompt: Make the real org-next/auth integration runnable locally. Fix the broken local script and env wiring, add the missing local callback and logout values or templates needed on both apps, and write a concise setup doc with the exact env vars, hosts, and manual verification steps for login and logout end to end.

suggestions.title: Preserve return_to through org-next login
suggestions.description: The current branch already built signed `return_to` support in `apps/org-next/src/server/auth-state.ts`, but `apps/org-next/src/routes/login.tsx` still calls `initiateLogin()` without the user’s original path. That means protected deep links still collapse to `/` after auth, which is the next obvious gap in this flow.
suggestions.prompt: Finish the org-next return_to flow. When an unauthenticated user hits a protected route, preserve the exact in-app destination through `/login`, pass it into `initiateLogin`, validate it as a relative path, and add tests/docs proving the user lands back on the original route after auth instead of always falling back to `/`.

suggestions.title: Retry org API calls after auth refresh
suggestions.description: `apps/auth` now exposes the app-mode `POST /apps/refresh` endpoint, but `apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts` still treats a missing or expired access token as an immediate 401-to-login path. The contract says refresh is auth-owned, so org-next is still missing the piece that keeps active sessions alive.
suggestions.prompt: Implement auth-owned refresh retry in org-next. On a 401 from the Org API, call the auth app’s `/apps/refresh?app=org-next`, retry the original request once if refresh succeeds, and only redirect to `/login` if refresh fails. Update the org-next auth docs and add focused tests around expired access tokens, successful refresh, and refresh failure.

suggestions.title: Add a real org-next auth smoke spec
suggestions.description: The repo has both halves of the new contract, but the tests stop short of proving them together: `apps/auth/tests/integration/app-mode.spec.ts` mocks a fake consumer origin, while `apps/org-next/tests/auth-flow.spec.ts` only intercepts the redirect out to auth. A single cross-app smoke spec would close the main confidence gap before more auth work lands.
suggestions.prompt: Add one real cross-app smoke test for org-next and the auth app. Spin both apps in test mode, drive a browser through org-next login, verify the auth app writes the signed `authToken`/`refreshToken`/`idToken` cookies org-next expects, and confirm org-next can reach an authenticated route without test seeding. Keep the setup minimal but runnable locally.

suggestions.title: Refresh expired org-next sessions instead of sending users back to login
suggestions.description: Today’s auth-app integration writes a `refreshToken` cookie, but org-next still only reads `authToken` and redirects on any 401. Codex can wire the auth app’s refresh contract into org-next so expired access tokens recover silently.
suggestions.prompt: Implement refresh-token handling in `apps/org-next` for the new auth-app integration. Use the existing `refreshToken` cookie and the auth app refresh contract so an expired Org API access token retries once before redirecting to `/login`. Update the org-next docs and add Playwright coverage for an expired session that refreshes successfully.

suggestions.title: Make organization cards actually switch the selected tenant
suggestions.description: The `/organizations` page looks like a tenant switcher, but clicking a card only navigates to `/` and never updates `selectedTenantId`. Codex can fix the mismatch with the sidebar behavior and add a regression test for multi-tenant users.
suggestions.prompt: Fix the tenant-switch flow on the `/organizations` page in `apps/org-next`. When a user clicks an organization card, persist that tenant through the user session before navigating home, and add a Playwright test that proves the selected tenant changes for a multi-tenant user.

suggestions.title: Finish the APPS_SESSION_SECRET rename before org-next stops booting
suggestions.description: Your current `dan/374-local-dev` changes switch org-next auth cookies to `AUTH_SESSION_SECRET`, but `src/server/user-session.server.ts` still reads `env.SESSION_SECRET`, which no longer exists in `src/env.ts`. Codex can patch the remaining runtime breakages and make the env contract internally consistent.
suggestions.prompt: Finish the local-dev secret rename in `apps/org-next` and `apps/virtual-terminal`. Find every remaining place that still expects `SESSION_SECRET` or the old per-app secret shape, patch the code so both apps boot cleanly with the new env contract, and update the relevant docs/templates in the same change.

suggestions.title: Reconcile auth app integration docs and tests with the new shared-secret model
suggestions.description: The auth app now reads `APPS_SESSION_SECRET`, but `apps/auth/docs/app-integration.md` and several auth tests still describe or exercise `ORG_NEXT_SESSION_SECRET` / `VIRTUAL_TERMINAL_SESSION_SECRET`. This looks like an unfinished contract change on the same active branch.
suggestions.prompt: Update the auth-side app integration contract to match the new `APPS_SESSION_SECRET` model. Fix the stale docs and tests under `apps/auth` so they describe and verify the actual runtime behavior, and call out any consumer-side assumptions that still need to stay per-app.

suggestions.title: Make virtual-terminal local env files match the app it actually is now
suggestions.description: `apps/virtual-terminal/.env.template`, docs, and `src/env.ts` disagree today: the template still uses `SESSION_SECRET`, while the code expects `AUTH_SESSION_SECRET` plus additional org-next-style vars. Codex can turn that into a working local setup instead of a dead template.
suggestions.prompt: Clean up `apps/virtual-terminal` local configuration so the env template, validator, and docs all agree. Keep only the variables the app really needs right now, preserve the auth-app integration flow, and make the README usable for someone trying to run VT locally on this branch.

suggestions.title: Harden the Amos demo payment route before this branch leaves your machine
suggestions.description: Today’s `dan/381-amos-demo` work-in-progress has two immediate push blockers in `apps/org-next`: the new demo route imports `payments.server.ts` directly from a route file, and tracked files now contain SubFi sandbox credentials plus a full render token. Codex can refactor the server boundary, move secrets to local-only env wiring, and update the org-next docs in one pass.
suggestions.prompt: Harden the new org-next Amos demo payment flow before this branch gets pushed. Refactor the route so no server-only modules are imported into a route file, move the SubFi credentials and render token out of tracked source/env files into the right local-only env setup, keep the demo working, and update the relevant org-next docs for the new payment route and config.
